name: inv_allows_empty_arch
reason: An architecture with zero layers should not satisfy the invariant, as a valid MMU must have at least one page-table layer.

name: aligned_zero_to_any
reason: Address zero being trivially aligned to every size could allow degenerate zero-address mappings to bypass alignment checks throughout the MMU spec.

name: inv_allows_unit_entry_size
reason: A single-layer architecture with 1-byte entry size and 1 entry is degenerate and should be rejected; x86 pages are at least 4K.

name: inv_allows_equal_consecutive_entry_sizes
reason: Consecutive layers having identical entry sizes (via num_entries=1) defeats the hierarchical structure; each deeper layer should map a strictly smaller region.

name: last_layer_max_entry_size
reason: The finest-granularity (and only) layer mapping 512 GB per entry is unrealistic for x86 paging; leaf-layer entry size should be bounded to 4K or at most 2M/1G.
